java code to compile hello world to native  exe for windows intel  platform using oracles native compiler

To compile a Java 'Hello, World!' program to a native Windows executable using the GraalVM Native Image compiler, follow these steps:

1. Install GraalVM which includes the Native Image compiler. Download the GraalVM Community Edition for Windows from the following link, select the Windows version (`graalvm-ce-java11-windows-amd64-XX.X.X.zip`): https://github.com/graalvm/graalvm-ce-builds/releases

2. Extract the downloaded GraalVM distribution to a directory of your choice.

3. Set the `JAVA_HOME` and `PATH` environment variables to point to GraalVM:

```powershell
# In PowerShell
$env:JAVA_HOME = "C:\path\to\graalvm-ce-java11-XX.X.X"
$env:Path += ";$env:JAVA_HOME\bin"
```

4. Install the `native-image` tool using the GraalVM updater: 

```powershell
gu install native-image
```

5. Create a 'Hello, World!' Java program in a file named `HelloWorld.java`:

```java
public class HelloWorld {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        System.out.println("Hello, World!");
    }
}
```

6. Compile the Java program to a native executable for Windows:

```powershell
javac HelloWorld.java
native-image HelloWorld
```

This will generate a native executable called `helloworld.exe` in the current directory.

7. Run the native Windows executable:

```powershell
./helloworld.exe
```

This will output:

```
Hello, World!
```

Keep in mind that the generated native executable will not have compatibility with JVM features like reflection or dynamic class loading unless explicitly specified. If your program uses such features, you'll need to provide additional configuration to the `native-image` command. For more information, see the GraalVM Native Image documentation: https://www.graalvm.org/reference-manual/native-image/
